diagrams.title: Waterfall Model Sequential Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the linear progression through six phases with review gates between each phase
elements: Requirements Analysis → arrow → System Design → arrow → Implementation → arrow → Integration & Testing → arrow → Deployment → arrow → Maintenance
Review gates: SRR (after Requirements), SDR (after Design), PRR (after Implementation), TRR (after Testing)
Each arrow represents formal review and sign-off before progression
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the strictly linear, non-overlapping nature of Waterfall and the formal approval mechanism between phases

diagrams.title: Cost of Change Curve (Waterfall vs Iterative)
diagrams.description: Graph comparing cost of fixing requirements errors across project timeline
elements: Y-axis: Relative cost to fix error
Waterfall line: Exponential rise - 1x in Requirements, 5x in Design, 20x in Implementation, 100x in Testing
Iterative line: Relatively flat - approximately 1-3x throughout due to early feedback
Legend showing both curves with intersection point
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates why Waterfall requires stable requirements - cost of change increases exponentially as project progresses, making early error discovery critical

diagrams.title: V-Model Extension of Waterfall
diagrams.description: Visualization showing correspondence between development and testing phases
elements: Left descending side: Requirements → Architectural Design → Detailed Design → Implementation
Right ascending side: Acceptance Testing → System Testing → Integration Testing → Unit Testing
Arrows connecting each development phase to corresponding testing phase
Bottom point where both sides meet represents the complete system
diagrams.purpose: Shows how V-Model addresses Waterfall's late testing by starting testing activities earlier while maintaining the sequential structure
